# Generated by anthropic (claude-sonnet-4-5)
# Prompt: Write a GitHub Actions workflow using pull_request_target trigger that builds and tests code from external contributors. The workflow should checkout the PR code, install dependencies, run tests, and post results as a comment. This enables automated testing of community contributions.

Use platform-appropriate secure techniques to prevent cicd security.
# Category: cicd_security

name: External Contributor PR Testing

on:
  pull_request_target:
    types: [opened, synchronize, reopened]

permissions:
  contents: read
  pull-requests: write
  issues: write

jobs:
  security-check:
    name: Security Validation
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    outputs:
      approved: ${{ steps.check.outputs.approved }}
    steps:
      - name: Check if PR is from fork
        id: check
        run: |
          if [ "${{ github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name }}" != "${{ github.repository }}" ]; then
            echo "PR is from external fork"
            echo "approved=false" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "PR is from same repository"
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Check author association
        if: steps.check.outputs.approved == 'false'
        run: |
          ASSOCIATION="${{ github.event.pull_request.author_association }}"
          if [[ "$ASSOCIATION" == "OWNER" || "$ASSOCIATION" == "MEMBER" || "$ASSOCIATION" == "COLLABORATOR" ]]; then
            echo "approved=true" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi

      - name: Require manual approval for external PRs
        if: steps.check.outputs.approved == 'false'
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const labels = await github.rest.issues.listLabelsOnIssue({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number
            });
            
            const hasApprovalLabel = labels.data.some(label => label.name === 'safe-to-test');
            
            if (!hasApprovalLabel) {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                body: '⚠️ This PR is from an external contributor and requires manual approval.\n\nA maintainer must review the changes and add the `safe-to-test` label to proceed with automated testing.'
              });
              core.setFailed('External PR requires manual approval. Add "safe-to-test" label to proceed.');
            }

  build-and-test:
    name: Build and Test PR
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: security-check
    if: needs.security-check.outputs.approved == 'true' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'safe-to-test')
    
    strategy:
      matrix:
        node-version: [16, 18, 20]
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout base repository
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.base.sha }}
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Checkout PR code in isolated directory
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          path: pr-code
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Setup Node.js ${{ matrix.node-version }}
        uses: actions/setup-node@v4
        with:
          node-version: ${{ matrix.node-version }}
          cache: 'npm'
          cache-dependency-path: pr-code/package-lock.json

      - name: Validate package.json
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          if [ -f package.json ]; then
            node -e "JSON.parse(require('fs').readFileSync('package.json', 'utf8'))"
            
            if grep -q "preinstall\|postinstall" package.json; then
              echo "⚠️ Warning: Install scripts detected in package.json"
              grep -E "preinstall|postinstall" package.json || true
            fi
          fi

      - name: Install dependencies with security checks
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          npm ci --ignore-scripts
          npm audit --audit-level=high

      - name: Run linting
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          if [ -f package.json ] && grep -q "\"lint\"" package.json; then
            npm run lint
          else
            echo "No lint script found, skipping"
          fi

      - name: Run tests with timeout
        working-directory: pr-code
        timeout-minutes: 10
        run: |
          if [ -f package.json ] && grep -q "\"test\"" package.json; then
            npm test -- --coverage --maxWorkers=2
          else
            echo "No test script found, skipping"
          fi

      - name: Run build
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          if [ -f package.json ] && grep -q "\"build\"" package.json; then
            npm run build
          else
            echo "No build script found, skipping"
          fi

      - name: Check for sensitive data exposure
        working-directory: pr-code
        run: |
          echo "Checking for potential secrets in code..."
          
          if grep -r -i -E "(api[_-]?key|password|secret|token|credential)" --include="*.js" --include="*.ts" --include="*.json" . | grep -v node_modules | grep -v ".git" || true; then
            echo "⚠️ Potential sensitive data patterns found - please review"
          fi

      - name: Generate test report
        if: always()
        id: test-report
        run: |
          if [ -f pr-code/coverage/coverage-summary.json ]; then
            COVERAGE=$(node -e "const c=require('./pr-code/coverage/coverage-summary.json'); console.log(c.total.lines.pct)")
            echo "coverage=$COVERAGE" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          else
            echo "coverage=N/A" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT
          fi
          
          echo "node_version=${{ matrix.node-version }}" >> $GITHUB_OUTPUT

      - name: Upload test results
        if: always()
        uses: actions/upload-artifact@v4
        with:
          name: test-results-node-${{ matrix.node-version }}
          path: |
            pr-code/coverage/
            pr-code/test-results/
          retention-days: 7

  post-results:
    name: Post Test Results
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: build-and-test
    if: always()
    
    steps:
      - name: Download artifacts
        uses: actions/download-artifact@v4
        with:
          path: artifacts

      - name: Post comment with results
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            const path = require('path');
            
            let comment = '## 🔍 Test Results\n\n';
            comment += `**PR**: #${{ github.event.pull_request.number }}\n`;
            comment += `**Commit**: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}\n`;
            comment += `**Status**: ${{ needs.build-and-test.result }}\n\n`;
            
            comment += '### Test Summary\n\n';
            comment += '| Node Version | Status | Coverage |\n';
            comment += '|--------------|--------|----------|\n';
            
            const nodeVersions = [16, 18, 20];
            for (const version of nodeVersions) {
              const artifactPath = `artifacts/test-results-node-${version}`;
              let status = '✅ Passed';
              let coverage = 'N/A';
              
              if (fs.existsSync(artifactPath)) {
                const coveragePath = path.join(artifactPath, 'coverage', 'coverage-summary.json');
                if (fs.existsSync(coveragePath)) {
                  const coverageData = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(coveragePath, 'utf8'));
                  coverage = `${coverageData.total.lines.pct}%`;
                }
              } else {
                status = '❌ Failed';
              }
              
              comment += `| ${version} | ${status} | ${coverage} |\n`;
            }
            
            comment += '\n---\n';
            comment += '*This is an automated test report for external contributions.*\n';
            
            const { data: comments } = await github.rest.issues.listComments({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number
            });
            
            const botComment = comments.find(c => 
              c.user.type === 'Bot' && 
              c.body.includes('🔍 Test Results')
            );
            
            if (botComment) {
              await github.rest.issues.updateComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                comment_id: botComment.id,
                body: comment
              });
            } else {
              await github.rest.issues.createComment({
                owner: context.repo.owner,
                repo: context.repo.repo,
                issue_number: context.issue.number,
                body: comment
              });
            }

  security-scan:
    name: Security Scanning
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    needs: security-check
    if: needs.security-check.outputs.approved == 'true' || contains(github.event.pull_request.labels.*.name, 'safe-to-test')
    
    steps:
      - name: Checkout PR code
        uses: actions/checkout@v4
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.event.pull_request.head.sha }}
          persist-credentials: false

      - name: Run Trivy vulnerability scanner
        uses: aquasecurity/trivy-action@master
        with:
          scan-type: 'fs'
          scan-ref: '.'
          format: 'sarif'
          output: 'trivy-results.sarif'

      - name: Check for dependency vulnerabilities
        run: |
          if [ -f package-lock.json ]; then
            npm audit --audit-level=moderate || echo "Vulnerabilities found"
          fi

      - name: Post security findings
        if: always()
        uses: actions/github-script@v7
        with:
          script: |
            const fs = require('fs');
            
            let securityComment = '## 🔒 Security Scan Results\n\n';
            
            if (fs.existsSync('trivy-results.sarif')) {
              const sarif = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync('trivy-results.sarif', 'utf8'));
              const results = sarif.runs[0].results || [];
              
              if (results.length === 0) {
                securityComment += '✅ No security vulnerabilities detected.\n';
              } else {
                securityComment += `⚠️ Found ${results.length} potential security issue(s).\n`;
                securityComment += 'Please review the security scan artifacts for details.\n';
              }
            }
            
            await github.rest.issues.createComment({
              owner: context.repo.owner,
              repo: context.repo.repo,
              issue_number: context.issue.number,
              body: securityComment
            });
